metadata.Name: Anonymous
metadata.If Yes, kindly specify:: But is undergoing a regular medicine intake due to low blood.
metadata.Prebiotics - Gut affectors:
Gut Affector 1 : Jeera seeds- 100g + Ajwain seeds- 100g + Caraway seeds- 100g + Flax seeds- 25g + Asafoetida- 5g + Himalayan pink salt- 5g.
Dosage: 1 teaspoon, twice a day after food (10g/day).
metadata.Stool Consistency: Type 3, Type 4, Type 6, Type 7 on Bristol scale

tokens.#TID020: These probiotic, immunity enhancing bacteria live in your intestines and help body fight diarrhea, constipation, and other intestinal disorders.By lowering its levels and supporting other beneficial microbes, we promote a healthier gut environment for smoother digestion and overall wellness.
tokens.#TID024: Blautia is a butyrate producer; supposed to have a probiotic effect on the body, Blautia contributes to regulating health by reducing metabolic disorders.Toning down an overgrowth of Blautia can help ease bloating and keep your gut feeling more comfortable.
tokens.#TID028: Lachnospira is a fiber-fermenting gut bacterium that supports digestive health and produces beneficial short-chain fatty acids like butyrate.Their overgrowth may contribute to anxiety, so maintaining balance is important and hence it is being reduced.
tokens.#TID032: This sweet sounding bacteria behaves as per its surrounding bacteria and nutrients. Mostly it is involved in degrading the mucin in the gut and it is seen that Dorea aids your digestion.High Dorea levels may contribute to bloating and loose stools. Reducing its abundance can restore balance, ease digestive discomfort, and promote comfortable bowel movements.
tokens.#TID036: Thse are part of bacteria producing short-chain fatty acids, especially butyrate, affecting colonic motility, immunity, anti-inflammatory properties and a lean biomarker built-in.Lowering this bacteria in your gut is part of our optimization strategy, which carefully balances it alongside other beneficial microbes to promote a healthier, more comfortable digestive system.
tokens.#TID040: Dialister often increases in people with fatigue and IBS, while low levels are linked to constipation, suggesting it helps keep digestion moving.Having moderate levels of Dialister in your gut may help improve digestion, support smoother bowel movements, and even reduce gut discomfort.
tokens.#TID044: A good bacteria with a prebiotic impact on your gut, Faecalibacterium is an important fella that produces butyrate, and propionate, maintains gut equilibrium, and good gut health.Supporting and maintaining this beneficial microbe can help reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote overall gut health.
tokens.#TID048: Catenibacterium is a gut bacteria that ferments carbohydrates to produce SCFAs. When present in higher amounts, it’s linked to more regular stool frequency, helping keep digestion smooth and comfortable.Increasing Catenibacterium levels can help relieve constipation, thanks to the SCFAs it produces that support healthy gut motility.
tokens.#TID052: The function of the gut bacterium Subdoligranulum is complex and still being researched, with both beneficial and potentially harmful roles identified. As a notable producer of butyrate, it is generally associated with better gut health.It is being increased because it is a top butyrate producer, fueling colon cells, keeping the gut barrier strong, and calming inflammation.
tokens.#TID056: An interesting bacteria commonly seen in the human gut, Collinsella is often a marker for the metabolic condition of the body. The bacteria ferments plant and animal origin carbs to produce lactate that converts to energy. It also plays a role in processing bile acids and can influence gas production, which may affect digestion and symptoms like bloating.A moderate increase in Collinsella can help support healthy lipid metabolism, contributing to better fat processing and overall metabolic balance.
tokens.#TID090: Gut diversity is the range of different bacteria living in your gut, showing how balanced and healthy your digestive ecosystem is.Low gut diversity means fewer bacterial species and weaker microbiome function. This can increase the risk of inflammation, metabolic issues, and infections.
tokens.#TID094: Insulin resistance is primarily the body"s reduced response to insulin and is unable to take up glucose from the blood. The microbiome plays a crucial role, contributing to the patho-genesis of obesity and pre-diabetes. A high abundance of insulin resistance bacteria shows a distinct pattern to carbohydrate metabolism and an increased movement towards metabolic challenges.
tokens.#TID098: Probiotic bacteria exert their beneficial effects through either inhibiting growth of pathogens, or production of helpful metabolites, reinforcement of gut barrier or positively modulating the immune system A high abundance of these bacteria contributes to various health benefits in particular realted to gut function , digestive health and reducing inflammation.
tokens.#TID102: Lactic acid bacteria (LAB) are microorganisms that can ferment carbohydrates to produce lactic acid. These bacteria produce myriad beneficial effects for human beings include, alleviation of lactose intolerance, diarrhea, peptic ulcer, stimulation of immune system, lowering cholesterol, and have antiallergic effects, and antifungal actions. However, a High abundance of LABs has been seen in those with gut inflammation, weight gain, insulin resistence and other metabolic disorders
tokens.#TID124: Dietary fibres, solube and insoluble, are not broken down by human enzymes but actually passed down to the colon where the residing bacteria work on them. These bacteria thereby produce multiple metabolites(chemicals) that in turn affect gut balance, gut barrier, impacting overall health, immunity, resistence to cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and more. However, excessive fibre fermentation/intake is seen with digestive distress ranging from constipation to diarrhoea, bloating, reduction in appetite and early satiety
tokens.#TID128: The interplay between the microbiome and your immune system includes multiple interactions in a state of balance and disease. Microbial signals ensure strong intestinal barrier function, maintain gut balance and elicit a protective immune response during an infection. A Moderate to High abundance of bacteria that enable better immunity provide protection to the body against inflammation and resistence to disease.
tokens.#TID132: Certain bacteria are responsible for breaking down complex carbohydrates, fibers, and other substrates into short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) and other metabolites that serve as energy sources for both the host and other microbial populations. Low energy producing bacteria can result in energy lags during the day, and disturbances to overall gut health and increased inflammation.
tokens.#TID136: The gut microbiota influences food intake by regulating brain function in a number of ways, such as by contributing to the production of chemicals which plays an important role in regulating gastrointestinal function, appetite, regulate fat storage, energy harvest, inflammation & circadian rhythms. Some bacteria can urge the body to store fat, thereby increase weight challenges. A low abundances of such bacteria can reduce their influence on your weight balance efforts.
tokens.#TID140: Protein degraders enable the breakdown of protein from animal and plant sources. They play crucial roles in the amino acid balance and gut function. On average, 6_x0096_18 g of protein reaches the large intestine per day that is roughly proportional to total protein intake . A Low abundance of these useful bacteria adds to inflammation, non-communicable conditions and poor health outcomes.
tokens.#TID144: Many complex carbohydrates, sugars and fibre are degraded and fermented by the bacteria in the small and large intestine to produce energy as well as impact health through the metabolites produced. These functions are primarily carried out by the bacteria as we lack the enzymes needed for this function. A Low level of carbohydrate metabolism can result in reduced SCFAs, impact inflammatory response, reduce satiety hormone levels, and even influence the intestinal barrier.
tokens.#TID148: The microbes in the gut play a huge role in cardiovascular health by impacting inflammation, regulating blood pressure, production of metabolites that influence lipid metabolism, and cholesterol levels in the blood. You have an advantage to have a Low abundance of bacteria that negatively impact your health health.
tokens.#TID152: The Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) ratio shows the balance between two major groups of gut bacteria that help regulate digestion and metabolism.A high Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) ratio means Firmicutes dominate.This may increase calorie extraction from food, contributing to weight gain and metabolic strain.
